item.title: How Mohammed bin Salman Has Wielded Saudi Arabia's Wealth | FRONTLINE (PBS)
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded:
And how some in President Trump's circle have benefited. Watch a video drawn from the new FRONTLINE documentary "The Crown Prince & the President."
This journalism is made possible by viewers like you. Donate to FRONTLINE now: https://bit.ly/47DFzCb
And support your local PBS station here: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Read more at pbs.org/frontline.
“The Crown Prince & the President” premieres in full June 30, 2026, at 10 p.m. ET on @frontline's YouTube channel.
“The Crown Prince & the President” is a FRONTLINE production with Rain Media. The correspondent is Martin Smith. The co-producers are Scott Anger and Jennifer Brooks. The writers, producers and directors are Marcela Gaviria & Martin Smith. The managing editor of FRONTLINE is Andrew Metz. The editor-in-chief and executive producer of FRONTLINE is Raney Aronson-Rath.
Explore additional reporting on “The Crown Prince & The President” on our website: https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/frontline/documentary/the-crown-prince-the-president/

Funding for FRONTLINE is provided through the support of PBS viewers, with major support from Ford Foundation, and the Fialkow Family Foundation. Hagler Foundation, and Corey David Sauer, and additional support from Koo and Patricia Yuen.

item.title: The Tinderbox: Understanding The Israeli-Palestinian Conflict
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
THE TINDERBOX: understanding Israel/Palestine We will never find peace unless we address the basic problem.
A rare and thought-provoking examination of both sides of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, from its catalyst to why it still rages, to what needs to happen for it to stop.
The Tinderbox combines ancient and more recent history, geopolitical problems (racism, inequality, immigration and fake truths), and psychology, with the deeply personal journey of one person forced to challenge everything they’d been taught about the conflict in Israel and Palestine.
The film also shows the human face of this conflict by hearing from a wide variety of contemporary voices, ranging from a Settler to a political member of Hamas. Today, while this conflict still rages, an American Middle East Peace Plan is in the works, and anti-Semitism and Islamophobia remain as globally controversial as ever, this film will provide context, show how history continues to play out in the daily lives of people on either side of the divide, and will empower audiences to make up their own minds.
From The Tinderbox
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more. item.title: Why are fans upset by water breaks at the World Cup?
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
CEO and Founder of the Men in Blazers Media Network, Roger Bennett, says many soccer fans are upset about FIFA imposing excessive water breaks for FIFA World Cup players. Bennett suggests FIFA’s implementing these to bump up its ad revenue, rather than out of pure necessity.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.author: BBC Earth Science
item.content:encoded:

item.title: Dogs and Their Masters

item.content:encoded:
Go to https://ground.news/horses to break out of echo chambers, understand different perspectives, and combat polarization with Ground News. Save 40% on their unlimited access Vantage plan.
www.horses.land

item.title: Banned weapons - Why Europe is bringing back landmines | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Landmines can maim and kill civilians and are banned in many countries under the 1997 Ottawa Treaty. Yet on Europe’s northeastern border, these controversial weapons are making a comeback.
Following Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Finland, Poland, and the Baltic states are rearming and bringing banned anti-personnel landmines (APLs) back into their arsenals. While military and political leaders say they contribute to strategic deterrence, mine victims and deminers say they pose a serious long-term danger. The war in Ukraine has reshaped Europe’s security thinking and triggered a radical shift in Finland. According to Colonel Riku Mikkonen of the Finnish armed forces, the military considers APLs a cost effective tool for national defence and a pragmatic instrument of deterrence. But to Zoran Ješić, who lost a leg to a mine in the Bosnian war, these weapons continue to kill, maim and render entire regions uninhabitable even decades after conflicts have ended. His story illustrates the long-term consequences of weapons that are banned by more than 160 countries. In Helsinki, Left Alliance politician Li Andersson opposes their reintroduction and warns against a sense of Finnish exceptionalism that could undermine trust in a rules-based world order, while Green MP Atte Harjanne argues that mines are a pragmatic way to protect Finland against a Russian attack. The documentary explores the tension between military deterrence and humanitarian consequences, asking whether anti-personnel landmines can ever be used responsibly.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. Subscribe and explore the world around you with DW Documentary.

item.title: Psychedelics, mind control, radiation-eating: The secret superpowers of fungi - Max G. Levy
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Dig into the vast and wild world of fungi, and explore how these organisms evolved to play a range of essential ecological roles.
--
The diverse fungal kingdom, which includes everything from baker’s yeast to edible mushrooms to foot infections, has long defied assumptions. Scientists estimate that somewhere between 2 and 12 million species of fungi remain undiscovered— either because they’re microscopic, live hidden within other species, or for other unknown reasons. Max G. Levy digs into the vast and wild world of fungi.
Lesson by Max G. Levy, directed by Petya Zlateva, Compote Collective.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------

item.title: AD - Why do I get this spark when I unplug my hair dryer?
item.author: Veritasium
item.content:encoded:
Why does a charger sometimes spark when you unplug it, even when it's switched off at the wall? We partnered with Anker to find out…
Pick up your Anker Prime Charger here: https://ankerfast.club/noagy5
#AnkerTech #Anker #AnkerPrime

item.title: The guilt you feel after killing bugs has a name
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Every summer, people on the East Coast are told to stomp the spotted lanternfly. It's invasive, it damages crops, and scientists say killing them helps slow their spread. So why does it still feel bad?
Vox's Sally Helm looked into that feeling and found out it has a name: moral residue. The guilt you feel when you do something you think is right but that still doesn't sit well with you.
Listen wherever you get your podcasts.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.title: Young people redefining success in China | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
They were raised on the promise of the Chinese Dream: study hard, get a good job, and build a better life than your parents.
But as China's economy slows and opportunities become harder to find, many young people are redefining what success means. Some are pushing harder than ever to survive, while others opting out completely. What do young people in China dream of now?
Full documentary: https://youtu.be/PovKEKm61x0
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
We kindly ask viewers to read and stick to the DW netiquette policy on our channel: https://p.dw.com/p/MF1G

item.title: Sustainable Augmented Development • Kent Beck • YOW! 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at YOW! Australia 2025. #GOTOcon #YOW https://yowcon.com
Kent Beck - Software Engineer & Creator of Extreme Programming @KentLBeck
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/kentbeck.bsky.social https://www.kentbeck.com https://github.com/KentBeck https://twitter.com/KentBeck https://www.linkedin.com/in/kentbeck https://tidyfirst.substack.com/about
ABSTRACT Genies are great at cranking out features. Unfortunately they don’t clean the kitchen as they cook. Progress slows then stops as yesterday’s expedient decision becomes today’s roadblock.
What would it take to get the trajectory in red instead of the one in blue? • Development in small steps validated by automated tests • Periodic pauses in feature development to improve structure and thus increase optionality • Potentially a new approach to augmented coding manipulating the tree structure of the code, not just the tokens
Sound familiar? [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 06:26 Cello-Story 08:10 Programming in uncertain times 13:03 3X: Explore Expand Extract 27:20 Feature drag 43:52 Junior developer: Profit over time 49:25 Closing thoughts 58:26 Outro
Read the full abstract here: https://yowcon.com/brisbane-2025/sessions/3896
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Kent Beck • Tidy First? • https://amzn.to/4gscjjK Kent Beck & Cynthia Andres • Extreme Programming Explained • https://amzn.to/3sBASDG Kent Beck • Test Driven Development • https://amzn.to/3U4AXLs Kent Beck, Fowler, John, William, Don & Gamma • Refactoring • https://amzn.to/3SFBYbN Kent Beck • Implementation Patterns • https://amzn.to/3sBlCGL
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #SustainableDevelopment #SoftwareDesign #XP #TDD #JUnit #EmpiricalDesign #ExtremeProgramming #Explore #Expand #Extract #3X #Ergodicity #Coupling #ConstantinesEquation #TodayInTech #Programming #SoftwareEngineering #KentBeck
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Black Americans and the Revolutionary War | Full Documentary
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
In 1776, the Founders’ promise of “all men are created equal” remained distant for many. Trace the heroic stories of the enslaved and freed Black Americans who fought to define democracy and their liberty through the Revolutionary War.
“Declarations: Black Americans and the Revolutionary War” includes visuals generated with the help of AI tools to supplement historical depictions. These are based on the work of a fine artist and clearly distinguished in the film by a rough, black frame. Learn more from the filmmakers about why and how they used AI: https://to.pbs.org/4eJ3Qci
Declarations: Black Americans and the Revolutionary War | Full Documentary
This program is made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Subscribe to the PBS channel for more clips: https://www.youtube.com/@PBS
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app: https://to.pbs.org/2QbtzhR
FOLLOW US:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PBS/ X: https://twitter.com/PBS/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/PBS/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@pbs Threads: https://www.threads.net/@pbs
#revolutionarywar #warhistory #blackhistory
Declarations: Black Americans and the Revolutionary War Follow the stories of four Black Americans as they navigate our country’s fight for independence and their individual pursuits of freedom. Discover the stories of James Lafayette, who served as a double agent for the Patriots and helped bring down the British at Yorktown; Harry Washington, who was enslaved by George Washington and escaped Mount Vernon to join the British troops; Elizabeth Freeman, previously known as Mum Bet, who sued one of the most prominent patriots in Massachusetts while living under his roof; and Abraham Peyton Skipwith, an enslaved man who used his proximity to power and his unusual literacy to petition for his own freedom and become one of the first free Black landowners in Richmond, VA.

item.title: How the Supreme Court just made Trump the most powerful president in generations
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Trump v. Slaughter, one of two “unitary executive” cases that the Supreme Court handed down on Monday, is the culmination of a nearly 40-year fight by Republican judges to expand the powers of the presidency. It transforms President Donald Trump into the most powerful figure to govern the United States in generations.
Read more: https://www.vox.com/politics/493610/supreme-court-trump-slaughter-cook-unitary-executive

item.title: Scientists Unearth 13,000-Year-Old Mystery Beneath California Coast
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
Locked away in a museum basement and wrapped in a protective plaster jacket for decades, this massive block of earth held the ultimate historical secret. In this premium documentary, follow world-class forensic scientists as they open the matrix to analyze the 13,000-year-old remains of Arlington Springs Man. This single discovery shattered decades of mainstream archaeological consensus, completely dismantling the "Clovis First" theory and proving that ice age humans colonized North America via an ancient maritime highway.
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more. item.title: Medieval Historian Reacts To Robin Hood On Screen
item.author: History Hit
item.content:encoded:
What does a medieval historian think of these portrayals of Robin Hood in film and tv shows?
Medieval historian, Matt Lewis delves into the history behind some of Hollywood's most iconic depictions of Robin Hood, sorting fact from A LOT of fiction...

item.title: Should Pluto Be a Planet Again?
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
Will Pluto ever be a planet again? Check out Ouranos with this link to find the best stargazing conditions and make the most of your next observing session: http://ouranos-app.com/download - start with a 7 day-free trial or grab 30% off an annual subscription
▀▀▀▀▀▀
This Astrum supercut explores our favourite dwarf planet: Pluto. We’ll discover why its planetary status really got revoked, take a closer look at what’s hiding under the surface, and reveal the surprising discoveries on its largest moon, Charon.
To those returning and new to the channel: This video is a supercut of some of Astrum’s best Pluto videos, plus new and updated content. We’ve edited this into a new seamless video, remastered in 4K resolution.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 Pluto 2:05 New Horizons Images 4:55 Pluto’s Heart 9:21 Subsurface Ocean 11:25 Cryovolcanoes 13:03 Charon 17:29 Double Planet 20:53 What Is a Planet? 23:14 The Kiss and Capture Theory 26:20 Charon’s Surface 29:36 Cryovolcanoes on Charon 31:55 Mordor Macula: Charon’s Red Spot 35:56 Will Pluto Be a Planet Again?
▀▀▀▀▀▀
To stay on top of space news, sign up to the Astrum newsletter: https://astrumspace.kit.com
A huge thanks to our Patreons who help make these videos possible. Sign-up here to support the channel: https://bit.ly/4aiJZNF
Astrum Displate Posters: https://displate.com/artist/astrumspace?art=5f04759ac338b Astrum Merch: https://astrum-shop.fourthwall.com/
Join us on the Astrum discord: https://discord.gg/TKw8Hpvtv8
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Astrum Podcast on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/6jPRrbq3o3dpvBb173ZTKi?si=a90d3efe3b704c83
Astrum Earth: https://youtube.com/@astrumearth Astrum Extra: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumextra
Astrum Spanish: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumespanol Astrum Portuguese: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UChn_-OwvV63mr1yeUGvH-BQ
▀▀▀▀▀▀
References: “Could Pluto Become a Planet Again?”, via bbc.co.uk https://astrumspace.info/plutoplanet “New Horizons Mission”, via science.nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/newhorizons “New Horizons Images of Pluto”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/nh_pluto “What Defines a Planet”, via open.edu https://astrumspace.info/planetdef “Charon: Pluto’s Largest Moon”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/charonoverview “The Accidental Discovery of Charon”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/charondiscovery “The Dynamics of Pluto’s Satellites”, via iop.org https://astrumspace.info/plutosatellites “The Pluto-Charon System (NASA Lithograph)”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/plutocharon “The ‘Kiss-and-Capture’ Origin of Pluto’s Moon Charon”, via nature.com https://astrumspace.info/kisscapture “Differentiation and Cryovolcanism in the Pluto–Charon System”, via usra.edu https://astrumspace.info/charoncryovolc
▀▀▀▀▀▀

item.title: Why did President Trump want to fire this Federal Reserve official?
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded: Why did President Trump want to fire this official at the Federal Reserve? Following today's Supreme Court ruling, FRONTLINE correspondent James Jacoby breaks down the battle between the Trump administration and Lisa Cook.

item.title: The World’s Most Average Person
item.author: Kurzgesagt – In a Nutshell
item.content:encoded:
If you averaged all 8 billion people together, what would you get? Meet the world’s most average person: where he lives, what he earns, what he eats, and how he’ll change over the next century.
#kurzgesagt #inanutshell #kurzgesagt_inanutshell #learnwithshorts #science #average #statisticsvideolectures #statistics

item.title: Dems are too dependent on the rich
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Are Democrats too dependent on big money?
Senator Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) says his party has struggled to articulate a compelling narrative and to break away from corporate power. The "technocratic" approach isn't winning over voters, he says.
Listen to the full discussion with The Gray Area host Sean Illing wherever you get your podcasts.
#Politics #DemocraticParty #ChrisMurphy #Vox #TheGrayArea #PoliticalStrategy #USPolitics

item.title: Democrats don't know how to fix America’s spiritual crisis | The Gray Area
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Sean talks with Connecticut Senator Chris Murphy about the crisis lurking beneath America's political dysfunction. Murphy’s new book “Crisis of the Common Good” argues that the country is suffering from a collapse of connection, belonging, and purpose. They discuss loneliness, powerlessness, liberalism, democracy, Trumpism, corporate power, social media, and why so many Americans feel disconnected from their communities, their institutions, and each other.
Subscribe to our channel! http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Host: Sean Illing (@seanilling) Guest: Sen. Chris Murphy (@ChrisMurphyCT)
00:00 Intro 01:11 America’s ‘spiritual sickness’ 06:34 What does it mean to pursue the ‘common good?’ 10:33 In America, the powerful profit from ‘the disintegration of the broader populace’ 12:14 The story Trump is telling America 16:25 How does the Right diagnosis America’s spiritual crisis? 23:14 Why the Left’s rhetoric isn’t landing 36:35 Bernie Sanders and the Democratic Party 38:48 Why AI is the most important policy fight of the future
We would love to hear from you. To tell us what you thought of this episode, email us at thegrayarea@vox.com or leave us a voicemail at 1-800-214-5749. Your comments and questions help us make a better show.
And you can watch new episodes of The Gray Area on YouTube. New episodes drop every Monday and Friday.
Listen to The Gray Area ad-free by becoming a Vox Member: vox.com/members.
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on Facebook: http://goo.gl/U2g06o Or Twitter: http://goo.gl/XFrZ5H

item.title: Platforms: Build Abstractions, not Illusions • Gregor Hohpe • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Gregor Hohpe - Author of "Platform Strategy", "The Software Architect Elevator", et al.
RESOURCES https://twitter.com/ghohpe https://www.linkedin.com/in/ghohpe http://eaipatterns.com https://architectelevator.com
Links https://twitter.com/ghohpe/status/1513362076990803969 https://github.com/97-things/97-things-every-programmer-should-know https://youtu.be/Zrj7RD7G24Q https://www.thingiverse.com/thing:6513
ABSTRACT Let’s be honest, the tech we use today is amazing, but it can also be complex.
It’s only natural that teams want to build platforms that hide this complexity to improve productivity, avoid mistakes, and reduce cognitive load. But they may be misled to believe that the more complexity they hide, the better their platform is. Instead, they end up creating dangerous illusions!
This talk reflects on two decades of building complex distributed systems, highlighting where abstractions helped and where illusions led to major disappointments. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 02:03 We shifted everything so far left, it piled up 04:12 Economies of scale & speed 14:23 Platform # IT service 16:0 Overcoming constraints 22:52 Standing on shoulders of giants 27:48 Abstractions vs. Composition 45:52 Outro
30:53 Build abstractions, not illusions!
Read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2025/sessions/3672
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Gregor Hohpe • Platform Strategy • https://amzn.to/4cxfYdb Gregor Hohpe • The Software Architect Elevator • https://amzn.to/3F6d2ax Gregor Hohpe • Cloud Strategy • https://amzn.to/3TOS3Nv Gregor Hohpe • Enterprise Integration Patterns, Vol 2 • https://amzn.to/3TNedQ3 Gregor Hohpe & Bobby Woolf • Enterprise Integration Patterns • https://amzn.to/3DqII9l Gregor Hohpe • 37 Things One Architect Knows About IT Transformation • https://amzn.to/3z8uhnw Kevlin Henney & Trisha Gee • 97 Things Every Java Programmer Should Know • https://amzn.to/3kiTwJJ
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #SoftwareArchitecture #PlatformEngineering #PlatformAsAProduct #ComplexDistributedSystems #DistributedSystems #PlatformAbstraction #ControlFlow #Abstraction #GregorHohpe #GOTO
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: You Live in a Supervoid
item.author: SEA
item.content:encoded:
Do we live in a cosmic void? It might sound like an odd question, but it’s something scientists have debated for decades. Does the Milky Way exist in a region of low cosmic density which is warping our view of the local universe as a result? The answer goes far deeper than you probably realise- and it’s just one of the many perplexing mysteries we’ll be diving into during this video, where voids and supervoids take centre stage.
Wow! That one was fun. Time for me to rest now and enjoy the sunshine.
It's official! SEA is now on Spotify! Stream new uploads every week! https://open.spotify.com/show/4xj3bcmXQYWL4g4r5cI8TS
You can now support the channel on Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/sea_media Patrons get ad-free access to videos, and also get early access sneak peaks!
Join my Discord Server: https://discord.com/invite/sea
SOUNDTRACK: The music tracks in this video were composed by Enzo Orefice, a royalty-free music creator. In order of appearance they are: - “Psyconaut” - “Submerged In Silence” - “Borealis Glow” - “Timeless Flow” - “Void Waves” - “Stellar Winds” Artist: https://www.music-bay.net/artists/enzo-orefice/
FOOTAGE: The space scenes in this video were captured using SpaceEngine Pro, a virtual universe simulator: http://spaceengine.org/ Get SpaceEngine on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/314650/SpaceEngine/
SOURCES OF INFORMATION / FURTHER READING: - Adam Moss, James P. Zibin & Douglas Scott — “Precision cosmology defeats void models for acceleration”https://arxiv.org/abs/1007.3725 - Ryan C. Keenan, Amy J. Barger & Lennox L. Cowie — “Evidence for a ~300 Mpc Scale Under-density in the Local Galaxy Distribution”https://arxiv.org/abs/1304.2884 - S. A. Pustilnik — “Nearby voids and their galaxies: recent progress and prospects”https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2022muto.confE..26P/abstract - R. Brent Tully & J. Richard Fisher — “Nearby Galaxies Atlas”http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/level5/March04/Virgo_cluster/frames.html - C. Carignan et al. — “HI Observations of two New Dwarf Galaxies: Pisces A & B with the SKA Pathfinder KAT-7”https://arxiv.org/abs/1601.00494 - ESA/Hubble — “Stellar Glitter in a Field of Black”https://esahubble.org/images/potw2020a/ - Robert P. Kirshner, Augustus Oemler Jr., Paul L. Schechter & Stephen A. Shectman — “A Survey of the Bootes Void”https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/1987ApJ...314..493K/abstract - Institute for Astronomy, University of Hawaiʻi — “X-rays Reveal What Makes the Milky Way Move”https://legacy.ifa.hawaii.edu/info/press-releases/kocevski-1-06/ - Yehuda Hoffman, Daniel Pomarède, R. Brent Tully & Hélène Courtois — “The Dipole Repeller”https://arxiv.org/abs/1702.02483 - M. Cruz, E. Martínez-González, P. Vielva & L. Cayón — “Detection of a non-Gaussian Spot in WMAP”https://academic.oup.com/mnras/article/356/1/29/1052311 - Seshadri Nadathur, Shaun Hotchkiss & Subir Sarkar — “The integrated Sachs-Wolfe imprints of cosmic superstructures: a problem for ΛCDM”https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.4126 - Matthew Williams / Universe Today — “Finally, an Explanation for the Cold Spot in the Cosmic Microwave Background”https://www.universetoday.com/articles/finally-an-explanation-for-the-cold-spot-in-the-cosmic-microwave-background - UK Research and Innovation — “DESI results suggest dark energy may evolve over time”https://www.ukri.org/news/desi-results-suggest-dark-energy-may-evolve-over-time/
Milky Way, Earth, Hubble Tension, Cosmic Acceleration, Hubble Constant, Supervoid, KBC Void, Hubble Bubble, Local Hole, Local Void, Void Galaxies, Pisces A, Pisces B, KK 246, Brent Tully, Bootes Void, Boötes Void, Great Nothing, Giant Nothing, Watershed Merger, Barnard 68, Great Attractor, Dipole Repeller, Shapley Supercluster, Norma Cluster, Peculiar Velocity, Repeller Void, CMB, Cold Spot, WMAP, Planck, Anisotropies, Eridanus, Eridanus Void, Eridanus Supervvoid, Integrated Sachs Wolfe Effect, Late Time ISW, Lambda-CDM, Dark Energy, Evolving Dark Energy, Cosmological Constant, Quintessence, Phantom Energy, Quintom Dark Energy, Equation of State, Big Rip
CHAPTERS: 0:00 You Live in a Supervoid 5:55 The Local Void 9:22 Boötes Void 12:06 The Dipole Repeller 15:54 The Mystery of the Cold Spot 23:12 Evolving Dark Energy 28:51 Future Efforts to Study Voids
Business Enquiries: SEA.Enquiries@gmail.com

item.title: Are loneliness influencers the new wave?
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Lana Isa is 24. She lives alone, spends most Friday nights in, and has no active social life. She also has almost 200,000 TikTok followers who can't stop watching her life of solitude.
She joins Vox's Noel King to talk about what her quiet life actually looks like and why so many people see themselves in it.
Listen wherever you get your podcasts.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.

item.title: Happy Birthday to the main man, Henry VIII.
item.author: Royal Armouries
item.content:encoded:
Happy Birthday to the main man, Henry VIII👑
Henry VIII transformed more than England’s politics, he also had a huge impact on English armour making. Determined to establish England as a centre of excellence, the king set up the Greenwich Royal Workshop, bringing skilled armourers from across Europe to create some of the finest armours of the 16th century.
Discover many objects from the Greenwich Royal Workshop here at the museum in Leeds.
Discover many objects from the Greenwich Armoury here at the museum in Leeds ⚔️
#HenryVIII #Tudor #armour #museum #history

item.title: Romania - Torn between work and home | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
In the 1990s, many Romanians left their homeland in search of work. They left behind deserted villages: children were raised by their grandparents, the elderly lived alone, and communities fell apart.
Antoanela Andonescu — affectionately known to everyone as "Coca” — also left her homeland back then. She worked for decades in Italy as a geriatric nurse. "The harder I work, the sooner I can return,” she kept telling herself. But months turned into years. Her sisters followed her, and home faded into a memory. When Coca finally returns to her small hometown of Bicaz in eastern Romania, she brings her savings with her — and a new sense of responsibility. She establishes a nursing home for the elderly who were left behind during the great wave of emigration. Almost all the residents in her nursing home have children abroad: Former cement factory worker Vasile has lived here since his family moved to Western Europe. During an outing with her residents to Lake Bicaz, Coca becomes pensive: "I don’t know how my life would have turned out if I had stayed.” Her own story is representative of an entire generation — torn between their roots and a new beginning. While Coca has found her way back to her native land, her nieces’ lives have long been rooted in Italy. In the Ostia region near Rome, where some 250,000 Romanians now live, a new home has emerged. Coca’s niece Andra has put down roots there — and wonders how much Romania will still shape her young daughter, Gizelle. What remains of one’s origins when life takes you to a new place? And what does home mean in a world where families are parted by continents? This documentary tells a story of farewells, new beginnings, and the search for belonging — and of people who, despite all the upheavals, are redefining their sense of home.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #romania ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. Subscribe and explore the world around you with DW Documentary.

item.title: Israeli Settler Surveils Palestinian Village
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: Why The German Army Feared The Night Witches
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
On the dark battlefields of World War II, German soldiers feared an enemy they could barely see and rarely heard... until it was too late. Out of the blackness came a faint whooshing sound. Seconds later, bombs rained from the sky, buildings crumbled, and chaos erupted across the Eastern Front. On this episode of Weird History we take a look at the mysterious Night Witches: an elite squadron of all-female Soviet pilots who became one of the most feared forces of World War II. Flying outdated wooden biplanes on daring nighttime bombing missions, they terrorized German troops, earned countless military honors, and cemented their place among history's most legendary warriors. Have you ever heard of the Night Witches? Let us know in the comments.
(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.)
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#wwii #nightwitches #weirdhistory

item.title: Surviving war | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Before Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022, Dmytro “Dok” Dokunov was a filmmaker living in an eco-community. While documenting his life, he was drafted into a combat unit of the Ukrainian Armed Forces and sent to the front. During the Kherson campaign, several of his comrades were killed. Promoted to commander, he was later deployed to the city of Bakhmut, where he was injured in urban combat, while many others never returned.
More than 55,000 Ukrainian soldiers have been killed since the start of the war, according to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and many remain missing. Those still fighting on the front lines carry countless invisible wounds of war. The psychological toll, and how soldiers cope with trauma, is of growing concern.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
We kindly ask viewers to read and stick to the DW netiquette policy on our channel: https://p.dw.com/p/MF1G

item.title: The MOST "Unsordid Act" in History?

item.title: Andrew goes to Palestine
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: Illegitimate children in Iran - Invisible and without rights | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Leyla loves her daughter Nila more than anything. But there’s a problem: according to Iranian law, Nila does not exist. Because she is an illegitimate child, she has no papers or rights. Mother and daughter must fight the absurd rules of a disingenuous regime.
But the resourceful duo refuse to give up. They hope to become part of Iranian society and save Nila from a life in hiding. "Nila's Dream in the Garden of Eden" accompanies Leyla in her struggle to achieve recognition of her daughter Nila's identity. Until then, she cannot obtain papers, go to school or receive medical care. The doors of Iranian society are closed to her.
Leyla and Nila desperately try to escape this cycle and free themselves from life in the shadows. They fight against bureaucratic hurdles and the opaque rules of an arbitrary state that is blind to the fate of a child. Despite everything, they manage to remain optimistic - they paint, sing, dance and fantasize about great journeys, a jet-set life of luxury and a miraculous twist of fate. They are a living example of how dreams and hopes can defy even the bleakest circumstances.
Film director Niloufar Taghizadeh knows Leyla from her school days. Years later, she learns of Leyla’s story, and a fate shaped by an absurd loophole in Iranian law. Driven by the desire to change the lives of the two, as well as that of many other undocumented children in Iran, she embarks on a risky journey to make this film. For almost three years, she accompanies the pair and films, partly undercover, in Iranian government offices and behind closed doors with decision-makers in the system to tell a story that, officially at least, doesn’t exist.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. item.title: The Truth about Space Data Centers
item.author: Real Engineering
item.content:encoded:

Links to everything I do: https://beacons.ai/brianmcmanus

Credits: Producer/Writer/Narrator: Brian McManus Writers: Malik Klalib Head of Production: Mike Ridolfi Editor: Dylan Hennessy Writer/Research: Josi Gold Animator: Ratimir Rakuljic Sound and Production Coordinator: Graham Haerther Sound: Donovan Bullen Thumbnail: Simon Buckmaster Head of Moral: Shia LeWoof
Model Credits: "Starlink Spacex Satellite" (https://sketchfab.com/3d-models/starlink-spacex-satellite-0a60f6720c5141c9a1c6d71aac108b31) by Malacodart (https://sketchfab.com/malacodart) licensed under CC-BY-4.0 (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)

Thank you to my patreon supporters: Abdullah Alotaibi, Adam Flohr, Henning Basma, Hank Green, William Leu, Tristan Edwards, Ian Dundore, John & Becki Johnston. Nevin Spoljaric, Jason Clark, Thomas Barth, Johnny MacDonald, Stephen Foland, Alfred Holzheu, Abdulrahman Abdulaziz Binghaith, Brent Higgins, Dexter Appleberry, Alex Pavek, Marko Hirsch, Mikkel Johansen, Hibiyi Mori. Viktor Józsa, Ron Hochsprung

item.title: Brexit, 10 Years On: What It Actually Cost Britain
item.author: Patrick Boyle
item.content:encoded:
Protect your privacy on the cellular level with Cape at https://cape.co/patrickboyle0626 Use code PATRICKBOYLE33 at checkout for 33% off your first 6 months.
Ten years on from the 2016 Brexit referendum, the UK is about to appoint its seventh Prime Minister in a decade — so was Brexit worth it? In this video we break down the real economic impact of Brexit: how much it actually cost the UK economy, why the doom-laden Treasury forecasts and the Leave campaign's £350 million NHS bus were both wrong, and what the data really shows about GDP, trade, the City of London, and business investment. We dig into the goods-versus-services split, why the people who voted Leave were hit hardest while the metropolitan elites worked around it, the great post-Brexit immigration paradox and the "Boriswave," whether Britain is really "poorer than Mississippi," and why both Brexit and the new "Rejoin" campaign rely on the same magical thinking. Finally, we look at Brexit as a global turning point — the first crack in the post-Cold-War order — and ask why ten years of arguing about Europe distracted Britain from the domestic problems actually driving its economic stagnation. Featuring research from the NBER, the OBR, the Bank of England, The Economist, the Financial Times and The Atlantic.
Patrick's Books: Statistics For The Trading Floor: https://amzn.to/3eerLA0 Derivatives For The Trading Floor: https://amzn.to/3cjsyPF Corporate Finance: https://amzn.to/3fn3rvC
Ways To Support The Channel Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/PatrickBoyleOnFinance Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/patrickboyle
Visit our website: https://www.onfinance.org Follow Patrick on Twitter Here: https://bsky.app/profile/pboyle.bsky.social
Business Inquiries ➡️ sponsors@onfinance.org

item.title: Missing people in Mexico – in the shadow of the World Cup | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Yesennia Aracely has been missing since March 2022. She was last seen near Akron Stadium in Guadalajara, Jalisco, one of the host cities of the World Cup. Jalisco is also the state with the highest number of enforced disappearances. 1
Yesennia is one of more than 100,000 people missing in Mexico, though researchers and activists estimate the real number could be much higher. 2 Families of missing people and search groups have criticized the government for the lack of support and attention, while resources are being allocated to the World Cup.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
We kindly ask viewers to read and stick to the DW netiquette policy on our channel: https://p.dw.com/p/MF1G

item.title: Young men are turning on Trump. Here’s why. | America, Actually
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Young men are no longer happy with Donald Trump. His net approval rating among the group that famously helped propel him to the White House in 2024 has dropped by 56 points.
But is this shift real, or just noise? Vox’s Astead Herndon recently went to an event billed as an opportunity for the administration to reconnect with young men to find out: the UFC Freedom 250 event in Washington DC.
00:00 - Is Trump losing young men? 00:58 - Inside the White House’s UFC event 01:25 - Gas prices and the Iran war 03:15 - Epstein files and a crisis of elite transparency 09:01 - Where masculinity and politics overlap 12:43 - Jack Posobiec on how Trump reached young men in 2024 16:58 - Defending the Trump administration’s foreign policy 17:49 - The Epstein dilemma 23:07 - Can JD Vance woo young men? 24:01 - The Blue Dog strategy 26:40 - What Astead took away from his trip to Washington DC
America, Actually publishes video episodes every Saturday tackling key issues in politics, culture, and the economy. Subscribe to Vox’s YouTube channel to get them. Listen to episodes of America, Actually on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite app.
If you enjoy our reporting and want to hear more from Vox journalists, sign up for our Patreon at patreon.com/vox. Each month, our members get access to exclusive videos, livestreams, and chats with our newsroom.
Subscribe to our channel! http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.title: Why Trump is losing young men
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Young men aren’t as sure about President Donald Trump as they once were. Polling indicates that men under 30 now have a disapproval rating of Trump that’s over 50 percentage points. Vox’s Astead Herndon went to Washington, D.C. to talk to young men attending the UFC fight on the White House lawn and see what concerns they have about the president.
Listen to the full episode of America, Actually wherever you get your podcasts or watch here on YouTube.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.title: Modelflabs B104 Round 2 review (beamsprings v2)
item.author: Chyrosran22
item.content:encoded:
Skip to 13:07 and 13:37 for typing demonstrations (solenoid off/on) Get it here: https://www.modelfkeyboards.com/product/beam-spring-b104-keyboard/ Today is a glorious day, we look at the new round 2 beamspring keyboards from modelflabs! It is a good time to be a keyboard enthusiast 8) . Hope you enjoy the video!
Intro by Kyle Carter Outro by Facundo Cabanne
My keyboard reviews: http://bit.ly/1TbOtft My switch teardowns: http://bit.ly/2C1QGHz My TOP X videos: http://bit.ly/2FmpZfd My XL typing demos: https://bit.ly/2OoAW3w My tutorials and featurettes: https://bit.ly/2OrkLUh My unboxing videos: https://bit.ly/2TSrr0m
I'm Thomas and I do videos and reviews on mechanical keyboards ranging from the most sickening modern RGB gaming keyboards to vintage hardware relics, or sometimes keycaps or keyswitches ranging from Cherry MX to Alps SKCM to IBM buckling springs and anything in between.
Follow me on Twitter for updates on my keyboard videos! https://twitter.com/chyrosran22
The practice sentence was: "Hello my name is Thomas and I'm typing on a Modelflabs B104 round 2 keyboard right now. This is an absolute joy to type on, and it looks beautiful! I just wish it was a little bit smoother."

item.title: Former adviser on Trump’s economic worldview | FRONTLINE
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded:
Stephen Moore was an economic adviser to President Donald Trump. He is an economist, columnist and television commentator. He previously held positions at the conservative think-tanks The Heritage Foundation and the Cato Institute.
This journalism is made possible by viewers like you. Donate to FRONTLINE now: https://bit.ly/47DFzCb
And support your local PBS station here: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Moore spoke to FRONTLINE’s James Jacoby on April 2, 2026, for our documentary "The President vs. the Fed." Watch excerpts from that interview above.
"The President vs. the Fed" is available to watch here: https://youtu.be/HbwIIQQ-yBE
Explore more of our interviews in this playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL_pPc6-qR9ZzEepVsKZsT58XiLb38Tttr
#Economy #FederalReserve #Interview
Subscribe on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/user/PBSfrontline Sign up for our newsletter: https://frontline.org/newsletter Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/frontlinepbs Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/frontline Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/frontlinepbs.bsky.social
FRONTLINE is produced at GBH in Boston and airs nationwide on PBS. The editor-in-chief and executive producer of FRONTLINE is Raney Aronson-Rath.
Funding for FRONTLINE is provided through the support of PBS viewers and by the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, with major support from Ford Foundation, and The Fialkow Family Foundation.
Additional support for FRONTLINE is provided by the Abrams Foundation, Park Foundation, John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation, Heising-Simons Foundation, and the FRONTLINE Trust, with major support from Jon and Jo Ann Hagler on behalf of the Jon L. Hagler Foundation, and Corey David Sauer, and additional support from Koo and Patricia Yuen.

item.title: How a lost giraffe revealed the dark side of exotic animal ranches in the US
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Gracie the giraffe escaped from a Texas ranch and became a viral animal story. But her escape points to a much bigger question: why are giraffes being bred, sold, and kept privately in the US?
Vox senior reporter Kenny Torrella looks into the exotic animal breeding, private ownership, roadside zoos, and hunting operations behind the viral moment.
Read the full story on Vox.com.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: Answering Questions About the U.S. Deal with Iran to End the War
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded: "Does the 60-day period given to negotiate the final deal mean that the parties can walk away from the table without reaching an agreement?” FRONTLINE correspondent @sebwalker answers viewer questions about the U.S. deal with Iran to end the war.

item.title: Historian Tours Britain's Best Medieval Castles | Full Series
item.author: History Hit
item.content:encoded:
Go to https://surfshark.com/historyhit or use code historyhit at checkout to get 4 extra months of Surfshark VPN!
We explored Britain's greatest castles!
Join History Hit's medieval expert, Matt Lewis, in this new series as he explores some of Britain’s most iconic castles. It is a spectacular journey to some of the best looking fortresses on earth.
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world-renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more.

00:00:00 Introduction 00:01:44 Bamburgh Castle 00:27:43 Ludlow Castle 00:56:19 Conwy Castle
#castles #medievalcastle #medievalhistory

item.title: Brett Goldstein was NOT Ready for this Audition | The Drawing Room with Mo Welch
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
When your interview subject is also your friend, something shifts. Brett Goldstein — co-creator of Shrinking, Emmy Award winner for Ted Lasso, and Mo Welch's actual real-life friend — arrives at The Drawing Room after a long day of filming, as ever the polite gentleman. What follows is less an interview than a reunion: two people who have traveled and had adventures together, now sitting across easels and pretending to focus on their portraits.
The conversation begins with the most urgent question: why two T's in Brett? From there it moves to the before times — the grimy London open mics, dying onstage at a place called The Lion's Den, sitting in the back with friends writing jokes and laughing about how badly it went. Brett misses it. Mo misses it too. For a moment, the studio might as well be that back room.
Then it’s the Mamma Mia! story. Specifically: how a young Brett Goldstein showed up to a dance audition in jeans, survived several elimination rounds by hiding at the back of a crowd of 100 leotard-clad dancers, and — when the choreographer finally came close enough to notice him — jumped over his own leg and hoped for the best. He did not get the part. He does, however, still remember the body ripple. Brett and Mo also talk about craft. Brett describes how he sees the world: "funny and embarrassing and awful and tragic and depressing and brilliant." He doesn't separate those things. It all comes out together, and that, he says, is why his work lands the way it does. Mo, who mentions she completed therapy ("they gave me my certificate"), asks Brett to diagnose her anyway. His verdict: she works too hard and loves too hard. She seems to accept this.
There are quick draws — Michelle Pfeiffer and Wario, respectively — movies that make them cry, and a running disagreement about whether Brett's portrait of Mo looks like a person or an apple. When they finally reveal their sketches, the friendship is right there on the paper — imperfect, honest, and genuinely funny.
Made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Subscribe to the PBS channel for more clips: https://www.youtube.com/@PBS
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app: https://to.pbs.org/2QbtzhR
FOLLOW PBS:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PBS/ X: https://twitter.com/PBS/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/PBS/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@pbs Threads: https://www.threads.net/@pbs
The Drawing Room with Mo Welch What happens when interviews become art projects? Comedian and illustrator Mo Welch invites notable guests to join her at the easel, turning conversations into creative collaborations. This non-traditional PBS series puts Mo’s full talents on display, blending humor, vulnerability, and artistry as she and her guest draw each other out—literally and figuratively.

item.title: Hitler’s invasion of the Soviet Union: Operation Barbarossa (2/2) | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
In early September 1941, the German Wehrmacht was advancing on all fronts in the Soviet Union. Kyiv had fallen, the Smolensk stronghold had been defeated and Leningrad was under siege. Hitler intended to launch a major offensive against Moscow before the winter.
Under the code name Operation Typhoon, the ‘final battle’ began - a desperate fight for the Soviet capital. The Vyazma-Bryansk double battle was aimed at crushing Red Army units around Moscow before conquering the city itself. Initially, the Wehrmacht managed to encircle and wear down the Soviet defenders. However, rain and mud then brought the German advance to a standstill. When frost set in after a good two weeks, troops were back on the move and the battle for Moscow could begin.
Thousands of civilians were drafted in to bolster defenses around the metropolis. Then Stalin ordered the evacuation of the city on 16 October. Panic and looting ensued. Three days later, a state of siege was declared and martial law was imposed. Despite major supply problems, a new German offensive was launched in mid-November, which ultimately failed.
By the spring of 1942, Soviet troops were able to regain large parts of territory lost to the Germans. The German defeat at Stalingrad in 1943 is also regarded as a decisive turning point in the conflict. Thereafter, the German front never recovered and entered a period of inexorable decline. Despite a desperate propaganda push, including a speech by Hitler’s propaganda minister Joseph Goebbels calling for ‘total war’, the German people had started to lose faith and doubt their nation’s victory prospects. Operation Barbarossa failed and ultimately led to Germany's unconditional surrender in May 1945.
Part 1: https://youtu.be/4FS34HAYgdo
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. Subscribe and explore the world around you with DW Documentary.

item.title: Jennifer Doudna Shares Her Pandemic Nobel Story
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
CRISPR pioneer Jennifer Doudna recounts receiving the Nobel Prize during the depths of the pandemic, telling Emily Chang the words her husband shared with her at that overwhelming moment. More on The Circuit https://www.bloomberg.com/news/videos/2026-06-24/the-future-of-gene-editing-with-jennifer-doudna-video

item.title: An industrial mystery in Brooklyn’s Greenpoint neighborhood
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
How can a company make 200,000 of something and become world-renowned, yet still go out of business?
This is Pin Point, the series where Vox producer Nate Krieger throws a dart at a map and investigates the surprising history of whatever address he hits. In this episode, he investigates an address where the most famous anvils in the world were made — until all of a sudden they weren’t.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.title: The Strangest Medical Stories We Could Find | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
The History of Medicine is a wild one. How humans figured out cures and even developed surgical procedures that saved lives! Often times what seemed to work in the past was later found to be detrimental to health and then abandoned. Today on Weird History we go into various aspects of the history of Medicine, the good, the bad, and the out-right miraculous! What is your favorite part about the history of all things medical? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
Chapters: 00:00:00 - What It Was Like Going To A Doctor In Wild West 00:10:11 - What It's Like to Be In an Iron Lung 00:21:34 - Bizarre and Bloody Practices of Medieval Barbers 00:33:22 - A Doctor Tried to Resurrect George Washington From the Dead 00:45:52 - What It's Actually Like To Have A Lobotomy 00:57:04 - The Crazy True Story Of The First Plastic Surgery In History 01:07:13 - Walter Jackson Freeman II The Champion Of The Lobotomy 01:22:33 - Horrifying Things We Used To Use As Medicine
#medicine #compilation #weirdhistory
